arguments.task: Open official Ramp documentation pages that support these fields for app research: product/API description, authentication methods, API reference surface, rate limits, and any official pricing or access/gating information. Prefer canonical first-party pages on docs.ramp.com or ramp.com. Read the pages and return the exact URLs visited plus concise extracted findings with quotations or paraphrases tied to each page. Do not use search result snippets. If docs reference authentication methods like OAuth, API keys, Basic, or tokens, list only those explicitly documented. Also check whether an official MCP page exists on Ramp docs or site.

input.thought: Start a browser run on official Ramp docs to collect first-party evidence for API, auth, rate limits, and access information.

raw_result.description: Financial operations platform with a developer API for spend management, bill pay, cards, reimbursements, users, and related workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Ramp documents OAuth 2.0 authorization with Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows on its authorization page, and the gathered evidence indicates token requests to /developer/v1/token use HTTP Basic authentication with client ID and client secret.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official Build with Ramp page indicates sandbox access is available for development, but production access requires an application/review process including materials such as a demo video and security documentation.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST API with multiple resource groups including accounting, bills, cards, reimbursements, spend programs, transactions, users, and webhooks.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Ramp has an official MCP page documenting Ramp MCP, Developer MCP, and Ramp Data MCP servers.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit appears buildable against sandbox/docs, but production use requires Ramp review/approval rather than purely self-serve enablement.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state a default rate limit of 200 requests per 10-second rolling window and a default timeout of 60 seconds.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly mention AI agent support and provide a dedicated MCP page, which is a strong signal of agent-friendly platform maturity.
